rootCause: When device restrictions profile with password settings is assigned after enrollment, Android does not prompt password setup. Dynamic device groups evaluate membership after enrollment, so profile is not applied until device is already enrolled.
solution: Assign device restrictions profile with password settings to device group before enrollment. If profile was assigned after enrollment, user must manually set a password meeting requirements for deployment to report success.

symptom: DeviceCapReached error or Company Portal Temporarily Unavailable during Intune device enrollment. User attempts to enroll new device but receives error indicating maximum devices reached.
rootCause: User is trying to enroll more devices than the device enrollment limit allows. The enrollment limit is configured per-user in Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Device limit restrictions.
solution: 1) Check device limit in Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Device limit restrictions. 2) Check user enrolled device count in Users > All users > select user > Devices. 3) Remove stale device records or increase device limit. 4) Consider using Device Enrollment Manager (DEM) account.

symptom: MDM authority not defined error during Intune enrollment. User sees: Looks like your IT admin has not set an MDM authority.
rootCause: MDM Authority has not been set in Intune tenant, or user credentials have not synced correctly with Microsoft Entra ID (UPN mismatch between on-premises AD and Entra ID).
solution: 1) Set MDM Authority in Intune. 2) Verify user UPN matches AD info in M365 admin center. If UPN mismatch: turn off DirSync, delete mismatched user, wait ~1 hour, turn DirSync back on.

symptom: Users with second verified domain UPN suffix cannot sign in or enroll devices when using AD FS 2.0 SSO. Only users with primary domain can authenticate.
rootCause: AD FS 2.0 does not natively support multiple top-level domains for UPN suffixes in a single federation service instance when SSO is configured with Microsoft 365.
solution: Deploy separate AD FS 2.0 instance per UPN suffix domain, or install AD FS 2.0 rollup (KB2607496) with SupportMultipleDomain switch.

symptom: Profile installation failed error during Intune enrollment. Device fails to complete enrollment process.
rootCause: Multiple causes: user not assigned Intune license, device already enrolled with another MDM, existing management profile, or browser cookies disabled.
solution: 1) Confirm user has correct Intune license. 2) Confirm device not enrolled in another MDM. 3) Remove existing management profile. 4) iOS: Safari default + cookies enabled. Android: Chrome default + cookies enabled.

symptom: Cannot create work profile on Android Enterprise device despite meeting OS version requirements. Personally owned work profile enrollment fails.
rootCause: Existing work profile already present on device, or OEM restrictions prevent work profile creation.
solution: 1) Check Settings > Passwords and accounts > Work for existing work profile and remove it. 2) Install Test DPC app from Play Store to verify device capability. 3) If Test DPC fails, contact device manufacturer.

symptom: Microsoft Edge sign-in prompt interrupts enrollment flow on Android devices (including Duo). User tries to access CA-protected data, Edge opens Company Portal website but prompts for Edge sign-in first.
rootCause: Edge displays its own sign-in prompt before the enrollment page when launching Company Portal website, diverting users from completing enrollment.
solution: Enroll via Company Portal app before accessing corporate data. If Edge sign-in prompt appears, skip it to continue enrollment flow.

symptom: Managed Google Play Last Sync time not updated in days on Android Enterprise device management console.
rootCause: Expected behavior. Managed Google Play sync does not auto-update; requires manual trigger.
solution: Manually trigger a sync from the Intune admin center. This is by-design behavior.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS devices show Management State as Unhealthy in Intune admin console. Devices are inactive and cannot receive policy, apps, or remote commands. Users protected by Conditional Access lose access to corporate resources.
rootCause: Device has lost contact with Intune service and cannot sync. Device may not be properly enrolled or has network connectivity issues.
solution: Open Company Portal app on device. If sync fails (Unable to sync), tap Set up and follow Company Access Setup flow to re-enroll. Successful sync restores healthy state and corporate resource access.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS enrollment fails with NoEnrollmentPolicy, APNSCertificateNotValid, or AccountNotOnboarded error. APNs communication channel to enrolled devices is broken.
rootCause: Apple Push Notification Service (APNs) certificate is missing, invalid, or expired. Steps to get APNs certificate were not completed or certificate has expired.
solution: Renew the APNs certificate (do NOT replace it - replacing requires re-enrolling all iOS/iPadOS devices). Verify enrollment is set up correctly and iOS/iPadOS platform is enabled. Then re-enroll the device.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS enrollment fails with DeviceTypeNotSupported error.
rootCause: User tried to enroll a non-iOS device, or the device is running iOS/iPadOS version older than 8.0.
solution: Confirm device is an iOS/iPadOS device running version 8.0 or later. Retry enrollment with a supported device.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS enrollment fails with UserLicenseTypeInvalid error. User account does not have correct license type.
rootCause: User does not have the correct license type for the configured MDM authority. For example, Intune is set as MDM authority but user has a ConfigMgr license instead.
solution: Assign the correct license type matching the MDM authority to the user. If Intune is MDM authority, assign an Intune license. Review iOS/iPadOS enrollment setup and user/group configuration.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS enrollment fails with MdmAuthorityNotDefined error.
rootCause: Mobile device management authority has not been set in Intune tenant.
solution: Set the MDM authority in Intune. Follow the Get started guide to configure the MDM authority before attempting enrollment.

symptom: ADE token sync fails between Intune and Apple Business Manager/Apple School Manager. Errors: expired/invalid token, access denied, terms and conditions not accepted, internal server error, invalid cursor.
rootCause: ADE token expired or revoked; Intune removed from MDM server list in ABM/ASM; new T&C not accepted; cursor expired or rejected by Apple.
solution: Expired token: renew token, may need to upload new public key to MDM server in ABM/ASM. Access denied: verify token not expired and Intune is in MDM server list. T&C: accept new terms in ABM/ASM with Administrator role. Cursor errors: contact Intune support to retry sync.

symptom: ADE Enrollment Program token upload fails with error: An error occurred while uploading the Enrollment Program token. Request ID: AjaxError: ajaxExtended call failed.
rootCause: Token upload failure. May need to regenerate the encryption public key and re-download the token from Apple.
solution: 1) Use Graph Explorer GET to enumerate tokens: /beta/deviceManagement/depOnboardingSettings. 2) GET public key for token GUID via /getEncryptionPublicKey. 3) Save as .pem file. 4) Upload .pem to MDM server in ABM/ASM. 5) Download new token. 6) Upload to Intune.

symptom: ADE device enrollment with user affinity fails. WS-Trust 1.3 Username/Mixed endpoint is not enabled in AD FS.
rootCause: WS-Trust 1.3 Username/Mixed endpoint (/adfs/services/trust/13/UsernameMixed) is disabled in AD FS. ADE enrollment with user affinity requires this endpoint to request user tokens.
solution: Enable WS-Trust 1.3 Username/Mixed endpoint in AD FS. Verify with PowerShell: Get-AdfsEndpoint -AddressPath /adfs/services/trust/13/UsernameMixed. For third-party identity providers, contact the vendor.

symptom: ADE enrollment stuck at user login screen after entering credentials. Initial setup does not proceed.
rootCause: Multifactor authentication (MFA) is enabled and the authentication method is set to Setup Assistant (legacy), which does not support MFA during ADE enrollment.
solution: Disable MFA and re-enroll the device. Alternatively, change the authentication method to Setup Assistant with modern authentication.

symptom: Government users signing in from another device during iOS enrollment are redirected to public cloud for authentication instead of government cloud.
rootCause: Microsoft Entra ID does not support redirecting to government cloud when signing in from another device. Default Cloud setting is Automatic which may detect wrong cloud.
solution: Open Settings app > Company Portal > Cloud. Set Cloud to Government manually. This redirects authentication to the government cloud.

symptom: Windows enrollment fails: This account is not allowed on this phone. Make sure the information you provided is correct.
rootCause: The user trying to enroll does not have a valid Intune license assigned.
solution: Assign a valid Intune license to the user in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center, then retry enrollment.

symptom: Windows enrollment error 0x80cf4017: The software cannot be installed, or The account certificate is not valid and may be expired.
rootCause: The Intune PC client software is out of date.
solution: Sign in to admin.manage.microsoft.com, go to Admin > Client Software Download, download and reinstall the latest client software.

symptom: A setup failure has occurred during bulk enrollment with Windows provisioning package (WCD or Set up School PCs).
rootCause: The Entra ID user accounts in the provisioning package (Package_GUID) are not allowed to join devices to Entra ID. These auto-created accounts lack permission in Device Settings.
solution: In Azure portal > Entra ID > Devices > Device Settings, set Users may join devices to Entra ID to All or Selected, and ensure all provisioning package accounts are included.

symptom: Windows Autopilot fails with Something went wrong OOBEIDPS error during OOBE.
rootCause: A proxy, firewall, or network device is blocking access to the Identity Provider (IdP) required for Autopilot.
solution: Ensure required internet-based services for Autopilot are not blocked. Review Windows Autopilot networking requirements and whitelist necessary URLs.

symptom: Autopilot device enrollment fails: Registering your device for mobile management (Failed: 3, 0x801C03EA).
rootCause: The device has a TPM chip that supports version 2.0 but has not been upgraded to version 2.0, or the device is in two assigned groups with different Autopilot profiles.
solution: Upgrade the TPM chip firmware to version 2.0. If issue persists, check if the device is in two groups with different Autopilot profiles and remove the conflicting assignment.

symptom: Enrollment Status Page (ESP) times out before sign-in screen loads. ESP shows loading but never completes.
rootCause: ESP is configured to track Microsoft Store for Business apps, and a Conditional Access policy with Require device to be marked as compliant is applied to All Cloud apps and Windows.
solution: Option 1: Target Intune compliance policies to devices so compliance can be determined before user logon. Option 2: Use offline licensing for store apps to avoid Microsoft Store check before determining compliance.

symptom: An error occurred while creating Autopilot profile. Device name template naming format does not meet requirements.
rootCause: The device name template uses incorrect format, such as lowercase %serial% instead of %SERIAL%, exceeds 15 characters, contains spaces, or consists of only numbers.
solution: Ensure naming format: max 15 chars, letters/numbers/hyphens only. No spaces, not all numbers. Use %SERIAL% (uppercase) for serial number macro or %RAND:<digits>% for random numbers.

symptom: Windows enrollment fails with error 0x801c0003 or 80180003: This user is not authorized to enroll.
rootCause: User has enrolled maximum allowed devices, device type restrictions block Windows MDM enrollment, device runs Windows 10 Home (only Pro+ supported), or Entra ID Users may join devices setting is set to None.
solution: Remove unused devices or increase device enrollment limit (max 15). Allow Windows MDM in Device Type Restrictions. Upgrade Windows 10 Home to Pro. Set Users may join devices to Entra ID to All in Azure portal > Entra ID > Devices > Device Settings.

symptom: Windows enrollment fails during OOBE Set up for work or school with error 80180014 when signing in with an Entra account.
rootCause: The device is recognized as a personal device and personal Windows device enrollment is blocked by tenant enrollment restrictions.
solution: In Intune admin center > Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Windows restrictions, create a restriction that sets Personally owned devices to Allow, and assign it to the target user group.

symptom: Windows 10 Group Policy auto-enrollment fails with error 0x80180002b in Event Viewer. dsregcmd /status shows AzureAdPrt=NO with incorrect TenantId and AuthCodeUrl.
rootCause: Device was previously joined to a different Entra tenant and did not unjoin correctly. Stale tenant info in registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\CloudDomainJoin\TenantInfo causes incorrect PRT authentication.
solution: Run dsregcmd /leave, delete device from Entra ID, unjoin from on-prem AD and delete computer object, rejoin to AD, run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta, verify hybrid join, re-sign in for PRT, then gpupdate /force.

symptom: Windows enrollment via Company Portal fails: You do not have the right privileges to perform this operation.
rootCause: The logged-on user is not a member of the local Administrators group. Enrolling an already-configured Windows 10 device in Intune requires local admin privileges.
solution: Use alternative enrollment methods that do not require local admin: Windows Autopilot, or join a brand-new device during OOBE (uses local system account).

symptom: MDM enrollment fails for co-managed hybrid-joined Windows devices with error 0xcaa9001f: Integrated Windows authentication supported only in federation flow. AADSTS50155: Device is not authenticated.
rootCause: Cloud Management Azure service is not configured in ConfigMgr, or AD User Discovery and/or Entra user Discovery is not enabled for the affected users.
solution: Configure Azure Services for Cloud Management in ConfigMgr. Enable both AD User Discovery and Microsoft Entra user Discovery for the target users.

symptom: After running Sysprep /generalize on a Windows 10 device enrolled in Intune/Entra ID, cloned devices share the same MDM enrollment certs, device IDs, and settings. Intune cannot distinguish cloned devices.
rootCause: Sysprep does not remove Entra ID join or MDM enrollment artifacts (certificates, registry entries, device IDs) during the generalization process.
solution: Never clone a device joined to Entra ID or enrolled in MDM. Create images in workgroup state, run sysprep /generalize before capture. Use Windows Autopilot for post-deployment enrollment.

symptom: Running gpupdate /force on a hybrid-joined Intune-enrolled Windows device shows warning: Windows failed to apply the MDM Policy settings. Gpsvc.log shows error 0x8018000a.
rootCause: The Auto MDM Enrollment with Entra Token GPO attempts to re-enroll the device during gpupdate, but the device is already enrolled. Error 0x8018000a means device already enrolled. This is expected behavior.
solution: This warning is expected and can be safely ignored. No action required. The device remains properly enrolled and managed.

symptom: Intune enrolled device shows UPN as None under Devices > All Devices after the device owner has been deleted from Microsoft Entra ID.
rootCause: Administrator removed or deleted the user from Microsoft Entra ID before deleting their enrolled device in Intune. Once the user is removed, Intune user information becomes unavailable.
solution: Use the RemoveIntuneDevice.ps1 script from GitHub (requires deleted user UPN, Entra ID recycle bin enabled, Microsoft Graph PowerShell module, and appropriate Graph permissions) to remove the orphaned device.

symptom: Help desk operators receive error Initiating Factory reset failed when performing remote tasks in Intune.
rootCause: The Help Desk Operator role is assigned to dynamic device groups instead of user groups.
solution: Assign the Help Desk Operator role to user groups instead of dynamic device groups.

symptom: When backing up an Intune-managed iOS device via iTunes, the Encrypt local backup option is automatically selected and cannot be deselected.
rootCause: An Intune certificate profile was deployed to the device. Certificates in profiles are flagged as nonmigratory keychain items, forcing iTunes to enable backup encryption (by design).
solution: This is expected behavior by Apple design. Encrypted backups ensure nonmigratory keychain items remain wrapped with UID-derived keys and can only restore to the original device.

symptom: RemoteWipe doWipe command fails on Windows 10. Event ID 400 shows error 0x80070032 The request is not supported for CSP URI ./Vendor/MSFT/RemoteWipe/doWipe.
rootCause: Windows Recovery Environment (Windows RE) is disabled on the client. RemoteWipe CSP requires Windows RE.
solution: Run Reagentc /info as admin to check Windows RE status. If disabled, troubleshoot and re-enable Windows RE.

symptom: Windows 10 device stops syncing with Intune after enrollment (2 min to 2 days). Manual sync does not start. No errors in logs. IME and PS scripts still work.
rootCause: The dmwappushservice (Device Management WAP Push) service is disabled, possibly by a deployed PowerShell script.
solution: Change the startup type of the dmwappushservice service to Automatic.

symptom: Disable Activation Lock action greyed out for iOS/iPadOS device in Intune, or bypass code not shown in Hardware overview.
rootCause: Bypass code expired and cleared, or device is not Supervised with Device Restriction Policy allowing Activation Lock.
solution: Ensure device is Supervised with Device Restriction Policy. Query code via Graph Explorer: GET .../manageddevices(deviceId)?select=activationLockBypassCode. Code valid 15 days.

symptom: Wipe action is grayed out for Android Enterprise personally owned Work Profile devices in Intune.
rootCause: Google does not allow factory reset of personally owned Work Profile devices from MDM provider. Expected behavior.
solution: By design. Use Retire instead of Wipe, or use Company Portal to remove work profile.

symptom: Windows 10 device cannot boot after Wipe from Intune, or BitLocker encrypted device fails to restart after Wipe.
rootCause: Option Wipe device and continue to wipe even if devices lose power was selected, causing boot failure with Windows corruption or BitLocker encryption.
solution: Use bootable media to reinstall Windows 10. Avoid the continue-to-wipe option unless necessary.

symptom: Passcode reset for Android 8.0+ personally owned work profile device returns Not Supported.
rootCause: Reset token not activated. Three prerequisites must be met on the device.
solution: 1) Require work profile passcode in config policy. 2) User sets work profile passcode. 3) User accepts secondary prompt allowing passcode reset.

symptom: Application not uninstalled from device after Retire action in Intune.
rootCause: App was not managed. Only apps deployed as Required or Available+installed via Company Portal are managed and removed on Retire.
solution: Verify app was deployed through Intune as Required or Available (installed via Company Portal). Side-loaded apps are not removed by Retire.

symptom: Intune Exchange Connector configuration fails with error 0x0000001: cannot connect to Exchange server.
rootCause: Internet proxy settings are misconfigured, blocking communication between connector and Exchange.
solution: Configure proxy via Netsh winhttp set proxy with correct proxy-server and bypass-list for internal addresses.

symptom: Intune Exchange Connector configuration fails with error 0x000000b: CertEnroll CX509PrivateKey Create file not found.
rootCause: Account used to sign into Intune is not a Global Administrator.
solution: Sign in with a Global Administrator account or add the account to the Global Admin group.

symptom: Intune Exchange Connector configuration fails with error 0x0000006: cannot connect to Microsoft Intune.
rootCause: Proxy server is blocking traffic to the Intune service.
solution: Remove proxy settings or configure proxy to allow communication to Intune service endpoints.

symptom: Intune Exchange Connector service fails to start with Event 7000 or 7041: logon failure for WIEC_USER.
rootCause: WIEC_USER service account does not have Log on as a service user right in local security policy.
solution: Run secpol.msc > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment > Log on as a service, add WIEC_USER. Check Group Policy override.

symptom: Exchange ActiveSync device not discovered by Intune Exchange Connector after sync completes.
rootCause: User primary SMTP address differs from UPN in Entra ID, or connector points to wrong Exchange CAS version.
solution: Fix primary SMTP to match UPN. For mixed Exchange 2010/2013, point connector to Exchange 2013 CAS. Verify Intune license.

symptom: Could not retrieve the access token for Microsoft Graph API when configuring Intune integration in Jamf Pro.
rootCause: Firewall or proxy blocks required TCP ports for Jamf-Intune communication.
solution: Ensure TCP ports 443 (Intune), 2195/2196/5223 (macOS), 80/5223 (Jamf Pro) are not blocked.

symptom: Jamf console shows Unable to connect to Microsoft Intune and Unable to send inventory information.
rootCause: Jamf license has expired.
solution: Contact Jamf Software for license renewal.

symptom: macOS devices marked as Unresponsive in Jamf Pro after Intune integration.
rootCause: Device fails to check in with Jamf within 24 hours, or Azure token refresh fails for 24+ hours.
solution: Enrolled user must sign in on device. Azure refresh token regenerates every 7 days.

symptom: macOS device fails to register with Intune through Jamf Pro due to app permission issues.
rootCause: Jamf Pro enterprise app in Azure has wrong/multiple permissions, or admin consent not granted.
solution: Remove default API permissions, assign single Intune update_device_attributes permission, grant admin consent.

symptom: macOS registration fails: Invalid command line input, Registration-only flag requires partner management enabled.
rootCause: Intune integration is turned off in Jamf Pro.
solution: Re-enable Intune integration in Jamf Pro via Cloud Connector or manual configuration.

symptom: Mac device shows compliant in Intune but noncompliant in Azure after Jamf integration.
rootCause: Device not registered correctly or registered multiple times without cleanup.
solution: sudo JAMF removemdmprofile/removeFramework, delete from Jamf/AzureAD, clean files/keychain, re-enroll via Self Service.

symptom: BitLocker silent encryption fails with conflicting Group Policy settings error in BitLocker-API event log.
rootCause: TPM startup PIN or startup key configured to Allow/Required conflicts with silent encryption.
solution: Set compatible TPM startup PIN and startup key to Blocked. Silent encryption requires no user interaction.

symptom: BitLocker silent encryption fails: TPM is not available, compatible TPM Security Device cannot be found.
rootCause: TPM chip missing or disabled in BIOS/UEFI firmware.
solution: Verify TPM via TPM.msc or Get-Tpm. Enable TPM in BIOS if disabled. TPM 2.0 requires UEFI BIOS mode.

symptom: Device encrypted with different method than Intune policy (e.g. XTS-AES 256 vs 128), causing error state in encryption report.
rootCause: Device was previously encrypted via different method (manually, MBAM, ConfigMgr) before Intune enrollment.
solution: Decrypt device manually or via PowerShell, then let Intune BitLocker policy re-encrypt on next sync.

symptom: BitLocker silent encryption not initiated: device ready but WinRE not enabled, or standard user lacks admin rights.
rootCause: WinRE disabled (prerequisite for silent encryption), or AllowStandardUserEncryption not configured.
solution: Run reagentc /enable as admin. For non-admin users, set Allow standard users to enable encryption during Autopilot to Yes.

symptom: BitLocker-encrypted Windows 10 device shows as Not Compliant in Intune even though BitLocker Drive Encryption is enabled and compliance policy requires device encryption.
rootCause: BitLocker encryption has not finished yet. Based on disk size, number of files, and BitLocker settings, encryption can take a long time.
solution: Wait for BitLocker encryption to complete. After encryption finishes, the device will show as Compliant.

symptom: Windows 10 device with Secure Boot enabled shows as Not Compliant in Intune when compliance policy requires Secure Boot.
rootCause: The Require Secure Boot setting is only supported on some TPM 1.2 and TPM 2.0 devices. Devices without TPM 2.0 or later show Not Compliant. TPM 2.0 requires UEFI firmware; legacy BIOS with TPM 2.0 will not work.
solution: Verify device has TPM 2.0 via tpm.msc. Check BIOS Mode is UEFI and PCR7 is Bound via msinfo32. Run Confirm-SecureBootUEFI in PowerShell. If TPM is 1.2 and device supports 2.0, contact manufacturer to update.

symptom: Windows 10 devices with Microsoft Defender Firewall enabled incorrectly show as noncompliant in Intune when compliance policy Firewall is set to Require.
rootCause: Known issue in certain Windows 10 versions (1809, 1803, 1709) causing incorrect compliance status reporting for firewall.
solution: Install KB4469342 (1809), KB4467682 (1803), or KB4467681 (1709). Workaround: set noncompliance grace period to nonzero days, or set Firewall to Not configured, then sync devices.

symptom: Cannot access company resources on Apple ADE (DEP) device enrolled in Intune with Conditional Access. Error: admin requires device to be managed by CompanyName.
rootCause: Enrollment profile has Authentication method set to Setup Assistant and Company Portal app is not deployed. Company Portal is required for Conditional Access enforcement.
solution: Change Authentication method to Company Portal in enrollment profile (Devices > iOS/iPad > Enrollment Program Tokens), or deploy Company Portal app via app configuration policies.

symptom: Error -2016281112 (0x87D1FDE8 Remediation failed) when deploying password/device restriction policy in Intune.
rootCause: For Android and Windows desktop, password policies cannot be immediately enforced via device restriction policies. Error persists until user changes password.
solution: Direct users to change password (Android: accept notification; Windows: Ctrl+Alt+Del > Change Password). Deploy device compliance policy for same password setting to enforce at enrollment or block noncompliant devices.

symptom: Intune-licensed users unexpectedly prompted to enroll in Intune when accessing M365 resources, even though Conditional Access not configured to enforce Intune MDM enrollment.
rootCause: Basic Mobility and Security for M365 security policies still deployed to groups containing affected users. Entra auth services check group membership and enforce CA based on BMS policies regardless of user authority.
solution: Remove affected users from groups assigned Basic Mobility and Security security policies, or remove the BMS security policy if no longer needed.

symptom: Script error 'Unable to get property Content of undefined or null reference' when logging in during Microsoft Intune setup in Configuration Manager.
rootCause: Login account is authorized for Delegated Administration on multiple subscriptions. Multi-account console is not supported during Intune setup on ConfigMgr.
solution: Use a Global Administrator account that does not have Delegated Administration permissions.

symptom: AADSTS50011 error 'The reply url specified in the request does not match the reply urls configured for the application' when querying Intune Data Warehouse via PowerShell or custom app.
rootCause: Default redirect URI for the application registration or PowerShell script was removed.
solution: For PowerShell: set redirect URI to urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob. For custom app: go to Azure portal > Entra ID > App registrations > find app > Authentication > add Public client redirect URI urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob.

symptom: 403 Forbidden error when querying Intune objects under https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/deviceManagement in Graph Explorer. Error: Application must have DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All or ReadWrite.All scope.
rootCause: Account used for Graph Explorer does not have required read/readwrite permissions to Intune device configuration and policies.
solution: In Graph Explorer, click 'modify your permissions' in the error, enable DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All and DeviceManagementConfiguration.ReadWrite.All, then accept permissions. If still failing, delete Graph Explorer enterprise app in Entra ID and re-consent.

symptom: NAC (Network Access Control) partner integration receives HTTP 503 errors from Intune when querying device compliance state.
rootCause: Broad unfiltered compliance queries are throttled after the first 60 minutes of a 4-hour window. After 60 min of unlimited calls, 3 hours of 503 throttle follows. Device-specific filtered calls are never throttled.
solution: Limit broad unfiltered NAC compliance queries to once every 4+ hours. Use device-specific filtered calls (by IMEI or Wi-Fi MAC) which are never throttled.

symptom: Help and Support blade does not load in Intune admin center. Error: login.microsoftonline.com refused to connect. Affects Chrome, Firefox; other browsers show similar errors.
rootCause: Browser is configured to block third-party cookies required by the Help and Support blade.
solution: Configure browser to allow third-party cookies: click the cookie-blocked icon in address bar, show cookies, allow each blocked site individually, then refresh the page.

symptom: Intune Troubleshoot pane shows 'Account status: You do not have enough permissions' for Help Desk Operator role members. No user or device data displayed.
rootCause: The Help Desk Operator user is not licensed for Intune.
solution: Sign in as Intune Admin, go to Users > All Users, select the user, assign Intune license on the Licenses tab. Then advise user to access troubleshooting portal in a new browser session.

symptom: iOS app shows App Not Set Up error when launching Intune-managed app with app protection policy
rootCause: No app protection policy detected for the app targeting the user security group
solution: Deploy an iOS app protection policy to the user security group and ensure it targets the affected app

symptom: iOS app shows Sign-in Failed error after user attempts to sign in with work account in MAM-managed app
rootCause: Failure to enroll the user with the MAM service after signing in with work/school account
solution: Ensure an iOS app protection policy is deployed to the user security group and targets the affected app

symptom: iOS/Android app shows Account Not Set Up error - organization has not set up your account to access work data
rootCause: User account does not have an Intune A Direct license assigned
solution: Assign an Intune license to the user account in the Microsoft 365 admin center

symptom: iOS app shows Device Non-Compliant error because device is jailbroken preventing app usage
rootCause: Intune detected the user is on a jailbroken iOS device
solution: Reset the device to default factory settings following Apple support instructions

symptom: iOS app shows Accessing Your Organizations Data error when user signs in with second corporate account on device
rootCause: Only one work/school account can be managed by MAM at a time per device
solution: Sign in with the pre-populated account or remove the existing MAM enrolled account first

symptom: Android app shows Failed app launch error with Intune app protection policy during MAM initialization
rootCause: App crashes during MAM initialization despite valid app protection policy
solution: Update the app and Intune Company Portal to the latest version. If error persists send logs via Company Portal.

symptom: Android app shows No apps found error when trying to open work data - no managed apps available to open content
rootCause: Intune cannot find any managed apps allowed to open the data
solution: Deploy an Android APP to the user security group targeting at least one MAM-enabled app that can open the data

symptom: Android app shows Unable to register the app error when app protection policy is required for MAM
rootCause: Failure to automatically enroll the app with the MAM service
solution: Clear the app data then send logs via Company Portal or file a support ticket

symptom: Users can copy paste text from Intune managed apps to unmanaged apps despite APP configured to restrict data transfer
rootCause: Restrict cut copy and paste between other apps setting is set to Any app
solution: Change the setting to Policy managed apps or Policy managed apps with paste in

symptom: Users cannot copy paste text between Intune managed apps despite both being managed by APP
rootCause: Setting may be Blocked or target document not opened from a managed location like OneDrive
solution: Change setting from Blocked to Policy managed apps. Ensure document is opened from OneDrive for Business or SharePoint.

symptom: Data transfer exemption configured but cut copy paste from managed app to exempted unmanaged app still blocked
rootCause: Cut copy paste restrictions are separate from send org data restrictions and cannot have exemptions
solution: Adjust Restrict cut copy and paste setting separately. Data transfer exemptions only apply to OS Share menu.

symptom: Exempted unmanaged app not appearing in share menu within managed app despite exemption configured in APP
rootCause: App exemption URL protocol or package ID incorrectly formatted for the OS
solution: For iOS verify URL protocol. For Android use package ID from Play Store URL. Test data transfer without Intune.

symptom: Intune app protection policy changes not applied to managed app - updates delayed up to 8 hours for signed-in users
rootCause: Intune App SDK checks periodically but changes for signed-in users may take up to 8 hours
solution: Have the user log out and log back in or restart the device. Ensure Company Portal is installed on Android.

symptom: Intune APP not applied on iOS MDM-enrolled device despite correct policy targeting - missing IntuneMAMUPN config
rootCause: Additional app configuration settings IntuneMAMUPN and IntuneMAMOID required when targeting iOS Intune-managed devices
solution: Configure IntuneMAMUPN and IntuneMAMOID for all MDM-managed apps. For third-party LOB apps also add IntuneMAMDeviceID.

symptom: Cannot transfer corporate files or copy paste corporate data between Intune managed apps despite both having APP
rootCause: The Send org data to other apps setting is set to None in app protection policies which blocks transfer to all apps
solution: Check the Send org data to other apps setting in Intune admin center and change from None to Policy managed apps or All apps as appropriate.

symptom: Users cannot receive files from unmanaged apps into managed apps - Camera OneDrive SharePoint data cannot be inserted
rootCause: The Receive data from other apps setting is set to None in app protection policies
solution: Check the Receive data from other apps setting and change to All apps or Policy managed apps. Also review Open data into org documents and Allow users to open data from selected services settings.

symptom: Users cannot save corporate files to local storage from Intune managed apps
rootCause: Send org data to other apps is set to None or Allow user to save copies to selected services does not include local storage
solution: Check both settings: if local storage saving is desired add local storage to the Allow user to save copies to selected services list.

symptom: URLs clicked in policy-managed apps fail to open - Microsoft Edge Required error displayed
rootCause: Microsoft Edge app is not installed on the device or not in the work profile for Android Enterprise
solution: Install Microsoft Edge on the device. For Android Enterprise WorkProfile scenarios ensure Edge is installed in the WorkProfile region.

symptom: SCEP certificate profile assignment fails when Trusted cert and SCEP profiles have mismatched assignment types (user vs device)
rootCause: The SCEP certificate profile and trusted certificate profile must be assigned to the same target type - both user or both device
solution: Ensure both the trusted certificate profile and SCEP certificate profile are assigned to the same target type (both to user groups or both to device groups).

symptom: Wi-Fi profile not deployed to Android device - profile pending certificates error in Omadmlog
rootCause: CertificateSelector from Company Portal does not find a certificate matching the specified criteria in the SCEP profile or Certificate Template
solution: Verify the EKU (Extended Key Usage) criteria in the SCEP profile matches the certificate template on the CA. Add or remove the Any Purpose option as needed to match.

symptom: Windows 10 Group Policy auto-enrollment to MDM fails with Event ID 76 error 0x8018002b
rootCause: Auto-enrollment triggered but failed - could be due to invalid license, misconfigured MDM scope, or Entra hybrid join issues
solution: Verify Intune license is assigned, auto-enrollment is enabled (MDM user scope set to All), MAM scope is None, device is Entra hybrid joined (dsregcmd /status shows AzureAdJoined YES and DomainJoined YES), and MDM discovery URL is correct.

symptom: Windows auto-enrollment task does not start - device already enrolled in another MDM solution Event ID 7016 error 2149056522
rootCause: The device is already enrolled in another MDM provider which prevents the auto-enrollment task from running
solution: Unenroll the device from the existing MDM solution first, then the auto-enrollment task will be able to run. Check Task Scheduler under Microsoft > Windows > EnterpriseMgmt.

symptom: Duplicate or orphaned mobile device management objects in Entra ID not automatically removed after 90 days
rootCause: Device objects in Entra ID may become orphaned or duplicated and are not always automatically cleaned up
solution: Use PowerShell or the Azure portal to manually remove duplicate/stale device objects: Microsoft 365 admin center > Entra ID > Users > select user > Devices > delete orphaned entry. Consider using clean up stale devices feature.

symptom: Windows Update ring policy conflicts with Group Policy causing updates not to apply or apply unexpectedly
rootCause: Mixed MDM (Intune) and Group Policy deployments create conflicts - old cached GPOs or SCCM local policies override MDM update settings
solution: Run gpresult /force to identify conflicting GPOs. If policy source is Local Group Policy check for SCCM settings. Edit the Active Directory GPO to remove conflicting values. Consider using ControlPolicyConflict CSP. Verify registry at HKLM SOFTWARE Microsoft PolicyManager current device Update.

symptom: MDM managed Windows device app installation error status 60 - code signing certificate not present or missing framework dependency
rootCause: App installation failed after download because the code signing certificate is not on the device or a required framework dependency is missing
solution: Ensure the code signing certificate is present on the device and targeted to all enrolled Windows devices. If framework dependency is missing, re-publish the app packaging the framework with the application package.

symptom: MDM managed Windows device app download fails with status 30 - device lost internet or enrollment certificate expired
rootCause: App download failed because device lost internet connectivity during download or the enrollment certificate expired
solution: Launch Company Apps from Control Panel to check if device certificate has expired - if so re-enroll the device. Confirm internet connectivity and retry the app request.

symptom: Wi-Fi profile is deployed to device successfully but device cannot connect to the Wi-Fi network. Certificate-based authentication fails silently.
rootCause: Certificate properties in the Wi-Fi profile do not match the actual certificate deployed to the device, or the RADIUS server rejects the connection due to certificate mismatch.
solution: Manually connect to the network using a certificate with the same criteria as the Wi-Fi profile. Compare certificate properties. Update the Intune Wi-Fi profile to match. Check RADIUS server logs for connection rejection details.

symptom: Users do not receive updated Wi-Fi profile after administrator changes the password/passphrase on the corporate Wi-Fi profile in Intune.
rootCause: Intune may not push the updated Wi-Fi profile to all devices after a password change on an existing profile.
solution: Set up a guest Wi-Fi network as fallback. Deploy the guest Wi-Fi profile to all users with auto-connect enabled. Test with a small group first. Verify SSID and credentials match by connecting to the Wi-Fi router directly.

symptom: All Wi-Fi profiles report as failing on Android Enterprise fully managed, dedicated, or corporate-owned work profile devices, even though some are working correctly.
rootCause: When multiple Wi-Fi profiles are deployed to Android Enterprise devices and one fails, all deployed profiles report failure status, including working ones.
solution: This is a known reporting behavior. Identify and fix the single failing Wi-Fi profile. Once the problematic profile is resolved, the other profiles will report correctly. Verify actual connectivity rather than relying on the reported status.

symptom: Wi-Fi profile reports as failing on Android device in Intune admin center, but the Wi-Fi connection is actually working correctly on the device.
rootCause: Reporting error in the Intune app causing incorrect failure status for Wi-Fi profiles on Android.
solution: Update the Intune Company Portal app to version 2021.05.02 or later to fix the reporting error.

symptom: Windows Update ring policies deployed via Intune are not applied to co-managed devices; updates not received or still managed by SCCM
rootCause: The Windows Update workload slider in co-management configuration has not been switched to Intune, so SCCM/ConfigMgr still controls update policies
solution: In ConfigMgr console, go to co-management properties > Workloads tab, switch the Windows Update policies slider to Intune (or Pilot Intune for testing). Verify the workload transition is complete before expecting Intune update ring policies to take effect.

symptom: Windows device stuck on earlier OS version and unable to upgrade via Intune Update ring policy despite policy being deployed successfully
rootCause: A conflicting TargetReleaseVersion value is set through Group Policy or Settings Catalog, pinning the device to a specific Windows version and blocking the upgrade
solution: Check registry at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate for TargetReleaseVersion value. Remove the conflicting GPO or Settings Catalog policy. Also verify UpdateServiceUrl and ensure DisableDualScan is set to 0 if WSUS is configured.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS Company Portal shows "Can't connect to server" error during enrollment or sync, even though the device has network connectivity
rootCause: A secure connection to the Intune service cannot be established due to SSL certificate issues. The customer's network configuration (proxy, firewall, or certificates) does not comply with Apple App Transport Security (ATS) requirements.
solution: Verify SSL certificates used by any proxy/firewall are valid and trusted by iOS. Ensure the network configuration complies with Apple ATS requirements (TLS 1.2+, valid certificates). Check that required Intune service URLs are not blocked by firewall or proxy.
